input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(releasing): author six-scenario rollback playbook (Story 4.1)

Expand the docs/RELEASING.md Rollback Playbook placeholder into a full
operational section covering seven failure scenarios, plus cross-reference
matrix and baseline-snapshots subsection. Update README.md Release Process
link to point at the completed playbook.

Scenarios:
  A - Bad version published to latest, no users yet (within ~minutes)
  B - Bad version live for hours/days, users may be installing it
  C - Bad version within last 72h, zero downloads, zero dependents
  D - Tag exists but npm publish failed
  E - npm publish succeeded but GitHub Release / tag push failed
  F - Suspected OIDC compromise / unauthorized publish
  G - release.yaml disabled, reverted, or missing from main (NEW)

Scenario G is new beyond the research-doc baseline, added per Epic 3 retro
commitment 2 - covers the single-rooted-workflow gap left by Story 3.3
Patch A (publish.yaml v* tag trigger neutralized).

All new gh api examples use name-based lookups (ruleset by name=="Default",
environment by literal release) per Epic 3 retro commitment 4. Existing
hard-coded ruleset/env IDs in unrelated sections of docs/RELEASING.md are
NOT modified; that cleanup is tracked separately in deferred-work.md.

Scope envelope: docs/RELEASING.md + README.md only (AC 17). No workflow
changes, no package.json, no CONTRIBUTING.md, no STABILITY.md, no
CHANGELOG.md.

Scenarios D/E/F are grounded in the Story 3.2 alpha-cut evidence:
bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0, run 24714953668, tag v0.10.1-alpha.0,
orphaned commit 2a57dcbd - verified SLSA-L2 provenance attestation.

Commit body uses AC N (not AC #N) form per Epic 3 retro commitment 10, so
the next release.yaml CHANGELOG append does not misinterpret numeric refs
as GitHub issue references.

Refs: AC 1-22, Story 4.1; unblocks Story 5.1 AC 1 precondition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

docs(releasing): author six-scenario rollback playbook (Story 4.1)

Expand the docs/RELEASING.md Rollback Playbook placeholder into a full
operational section covering seven failure scenarios, plus cross-reference
matrix and baseline-snapshots subsection. Update README.md Release Process
link to point at the completed playbook.

Scenarios:
  A - Bad version published to latest, no users yet (within ~minutes)
  B - Bad version live for hours/days, users may be installing it
  C - Bad version within last 72h, zero downloads, zero dependents
  D - Tag exists but npm publish failed
  E - npm publish succeeded but GitHub Release / tag push failed
  F - Suspected OIDC compromise / unauthorized publish
  G - release.yaml disabled, reverted, or missing from main (NEW)

Scenario G is new beyond the research-doc baseline, added per Epic 3 retro
commitment 2 - covers the single-rooted-workflow gap left by Story 3.3
Patch A (publish.yaml v* tag trigger neutralized).

All new gh api examples use name-based lookups (ruleset by name=="Default",
environment by literal release) per Epic 3 retro commitment 4. Existing
hard-coded ruleset/env IDs in unrelated sections of docs/RELEASING.md are
NOT modified; that cleanup is tracked separately in deferred-work.md.

Scope envelope: docs/RELEASING.md + README.md only (AC 17). No workflow
changes, no package.json, no CONTRIBUTING.md, no STABILITY.md, no
CHANGELOG.md.

Scenarios D/E/F are grounded in the Story 3.2 alpha-cut evidence:
bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0, run 24714953668, tag v0.10.1-alpha.0,
orphaned commit 2a57dcbd - verified SLSA-L2 provenance attestation.

Commit body uses AC N (not AC #N) form per Epic 3 retro commitment 10, so
the next release.yaml CHANGELOG append does not misinterpret numeric refs
as GitHub issue references.

Refs: AC 1-22, Story 4.1; unblocks Story 5.1 AC 1 precondition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 3b60461d8614

### Debug Log References

All Task 1 recon commands executed in parallel; outputs matched create-story-time
expectations exactly (no drift since 2026-04-21). Full `npm test` run — 12/12
subcommands green. Pre-commit hook ran lint-staged (markdownlint on 2 *.md files)
and the full `npm test` suite as the commit-msg hook; both succeeded on first pass.

Commit: `4d0c618` on `feat/release-workflow`.

### Completion Notes List

**Story-level accomplishments:**

- `docs/RELEASING.md` Rollback Playbook placeholder (lines 181-185) replaced with
  seven-scenario operational playbook (~320 lines added); section totals: banner
  (NFR6 immutability) + 7 five-element scenarios (A–G) + cross-reference matrix +
  `### Baseline snapshots` subsection (capture pattern + PUT-then-POST ruleset-deletion
  recovery path).
- `README.md:188` updated from `(later) the rollback playbook` placeholder to a
  direct anchor link `#rollback-playbook`.
- Full `npm test` suite (12 subcommands) green post-edit, including `lint:md`,
  `format:check`, `validate:refs`, `validate:schemas`, `validate:skills`, and the
  Python/pytest suite.
- Scope envelope held: `git status --short` showed exactly `M docs/RELEASING.md`
  and `M README.md` — no other files touched (AC 17 satisfied).
- Unblocks Story 5.1 AC 1 precondition (`docs/RELEASING.md` rollback playbook exists).

**Load-bearing decisions resolved at drafting time (Dev Notes § "Load-bearing decisions"):**

- **(A) Scenario G `state` field.** Live `gh api .../actions/workflows` output uses
  `state` as the active/disabled field name; authored Scenario G detection CLI
  against that field directly. Noted `disabled_manually` as the expected value when
  disabled via UI or `gh workflow disable` — not observed live (all 10 workflows
  report `active`), but the GitHub API doc surface confirms the value.
- **(B) Cross-reference matrix placement.** Placed at end of section per AC 10 — a
  leading ToC-style list under the banner was considered but the 7-scenario body
  reads better as sequential prose + end-of-section matrix than as redundant scan-refs.
- **(C) Baseline file path.** Used `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-*-$(date +%Y%m%d).json`
  (git-tracked, not shipped with the npm package) — matches the rest of the BMAD
  artifact tree.
- **(D) Scenario F provenance verification.** Included BOTH `npm audit signatures`
  (human-friendly quick check) AND `npm view @<v> --json | jq '.dist.attestations'`
  (script-friendly detail check).
- **(E) Scenario G emergency-hatch wording.** Cited `publish.yaml workflow_dispatch`
  explicitly as an emergency-only fallback with post-mortem requirement, per AC 9.e;
  flagged that this reference becomes stale post-Story 6.1.

**Flags for downstream stories:**

- **Starlight sidebar entry missing for `docs/RELEASING.md`** — `grep RELEASING website/astro.config.mjs`
  returned nothing. Deferred to Story 5.1 per AC 19 (shared with STABILITY.md sidebar
  deferral).
- **Story 5.1 edits** will follow the shape established here when adding
  `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` (Epic 3 retro H8) — adjacent to or following the Rollback
  Playbook section. Structure is consistent.
- **Story 6.1** (delete `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`) will need to edit
  Scenario G to remove the emergency-hatch reference to `publish.yaml` — replace
  with a pure `support@npmjs.com` + incident post-mortem path. Forward-flag noted
  in-story.
- **Story 6.3** (remove `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret) will need to simplify Scenario F's
  `Do-NOT` clause — token no longer exists, so the compromised-token-parallel-to-OIDC
  scenario collapses. Forward-flag noted in-story.

**No new deferred-work items surfaced.** All eight deferred-work items the story was
scoped to address (1-1 ruleset-deletion, 1-2 hard-coded IDs + baseline capture, 1-3
empty-Rollback-Playbook + NPM_TOKEN fallback, 3-1/3-2 tag-before-publish, 3-2 orphaned-commit,
3-3 release.yaml single-root assertion) are now covered by the corresponding ACs.
Existing deferred-work.md items outside Story 4.1's scope remain untouched.

**No CLI shape drift caught.** The Scenario G detection CLI output shape
(`{name, state, path}` per `.jq` filter) matches the AC and the live API exactly.
The ruleset `{id, name}` shape in Task 1's recon also matches the name-based lookup
pattern in AC 11.

### File List

- `docs/RELEASING.md` (MODIFIED) — 185 → 507 lines; +323 lines / -1 line; Rollback
  Playbook section expanded from placeholder to full operational content with
  banner + 7 scenarios (A–G) + cross-reference matrix + baseline-snapshots subsection
- `README.md` (MODIFIED) — 1 line changed at line 188; `(later) the rollback playbook.` →
  `the [rollback playbook](docs/RELEASING.md#rollback-playbook) covering seven failure scenarios.`

| Date | Version | Description | Author |
|---|---|---|---|

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)
